title: Fix jq path injection in pipeline-state write
description: The `write` action in bin/pipeline-state interpolates $key directly into the jq expression `"$key = \$v"`. The denylist character filter at lines 56-61 misses backtick, <, >, *, ?, ), {, }, &, \, single quote, double quote, newline. A crafted key could execute arbitrary jq expressions (including env-exfiltration via error() or input_filename).
acceptance_criteria: Keys are validated via allowlist of path components, not denylist of characters
pipeline-state write uses jq setpath with --argjson path, not string interpolation
Callers use dotted paths that are parsed into arrays before being passed to jq
Attempted injection keys (e.g., `. | env`, `test" + "injection`) are rejected with clear error
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-state write rejects key with backtick
bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-state write rejects key with jq pipe operator
bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-state write rejects key with error() call
bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-state write accepts legitimate nested path like .tasks.task_1.status
bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-state write with setpath correctly updates nested key

title: Fix JSON string concatenation in pipeline-validate-tasks
description: Line 183 builds JSON via string concatenation: `execution_order+=("{\"task_id\":\"$tid\",\"parallel_group\":$group}")`. $tid comes from LLM-generated tasks.json. A task_id containing a double quote or control character corrupts the JSON or enables injection.
acceptance_criteria: execution_order entries are built via jq -n --arg tid --argjson group, never string concatenation
A task_id containing embedded double quotes is either rejected (preferred) or safely encoded
Final execution_order array validates as proper JSON
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase3.sh: pipeline-validate-tasks rejects task_id containing double quote
bin/test-phase3.sh: pipeline-validate-tasks rejects task_id containing newline
bin/test-phase3.sh: pipeline-validate-tasks output is always valid JSON regardless of input task_id contents

title: Add path validation to pipeline-cleanup --spec-dir
description: Line 154 of bin/pipeline-cleanup runs `rm -rf "$spec_dir"` with no path validation. The orchestrator (an agent influenced by LLM output) can be steered into passing --spec-dir / or --spec-dir ~ or a symlink.
acceptance_criteria: spec_dir must resolve (via realpath) to a path underneath the current git repo's top-level directory
Absolute paths outside the project are rejected
Symlinks pointing outside the project are rejected (realpath resolution)
Empty, dot, and '..' paths are rejected
Clear error message on rejection
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase5.sh: pipeline-cleanup --clean-spec --spec-dir / is rejected
bin/test-phase5.sh: pipeline-cleanup --clean-spec --spec-dir ~ is rejected
bin/test-phase5.sh: pipeline-cleanup --clean-spec --spec-dir ../other is rejected
bin/test-phase5.sh: pipeline-cleanup --clean-spec --spec-dir pointing to symlink outside project is rejected
bin/test-phase5.sh: pipeline-cleanup --clean-spec --spec-dir valid in-project path succeeds

title: Validate numeric args in pipeline-init
description: pipeline-init:50 builds `[%s]` for issue_numbers without validating that $issue is numeric. A non-numeric issue value like `42,"poisoned":1` breaks the JSON or enables injection via --argjson.
acceptance_criteria: --issue arg is validated as positive integer before being used in --argjson
Invalid numeric input causes script to exit 1 with clear error message
run_id validation already exists — extend with similar strictness to all numeric args
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-init --issue abc fails with error
bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-init --issue '42; rm -rf /' fails
bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-init --issue 42 succeeds and emits valid state.json

title: Restrict pipeline-lock --pid flag
description: bin/pipeline-lock accepts --pid N from any caller. An attacker or errant orchestrator can pass --pid 1 (init) — the kill -0 liveness check always succeeds, making the lock un-reclaimable. Remove the flag or restrict it to caller's own process tree.
acceptance_criteria: --pid flag is removed entirely, OR restricted to PIDs in the caller's process tree (verified via ps -o ppid=)
Default remains $$ (current shell PID)
Test-suite usage of --pid is documented or uses a dedicated test-only env var
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-lock acquire --pid 1 is rejected OR only acquires if 1 is an ancestor
bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-lock acquire without --pid uses $$ as before

title: Fix bash octal interpretation in pipeline-quota-check
description: Line 69-70 of bin/pipeline-quota-check uses `hour_in_day=$(date -u +%H); window_hour=$(( (hour_in_day % 5) + 1 ))`. Zero-padded hours (08, 09) are interpreted as octal by bash arithmetic, causing 'value too great for base' errors between 08:00-09:59 UTC. Fix with 10# prefix (same pattern already applied in pipeline-model-router).
acceptance_criteria: All bash arithmetic involving `date +%H`, `+%M`, `+%d`, `+%u` uses `10#$var` prefix
Script runs without error at any hour of day (including 08:00-09:59 UTC)
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase7.sh: pipeline-quota-check does not crash when hour_in_day is 08
bin/test-phase7.sh: pipeline-quota-check does not crash when hour_in_day is 09

title: Rewrite quota window math to use resets_at semantics
description: Current pipeline-quota-check computes `window_hour = (hour_in_day % 5) + 1`, assuming windows reset at UTC 00/05/10/15/20. Anthropic's 5-hour windows are actually tied to the first request in a session. The correct formula (from 04-data-flow.md:489) is `window_hour = floor((now - (resets_at - 5h)) / 3600) + 1`. Similarly for 7-day window, the daily_threshold must be derived from resets_at - 7d, not from day-of-week.
acceptance_criteria: 5h window_hour computed from resets_at field in API/headers response
7d window_day computed from resets_at - 7d, not day-of-week
Hourly threshold = min(window_hour * 0.20, 0.90)
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase7.sh: window_hour=1 when now is 10 minutes into the window
bin/test-phase7.sh: window_hour=5 when now is 4.5 hours into the window
bin/test-phase7.sh: window_day=4 when now is 3.5 days into a 7-day window
bin/test-phase7.sh: daily_threshold on day 7 is 0.95 not 1.0
bin/test-phase7.sh: hourly_threshold caps at 0.90 for window_hour=5

title: Implement header-based detection from last-headers.json
description: `_check_headers()` at pipeline-quota-check:157-162 is a stub. Decision 10 in 05-decisions.md mandates reading unified-5h-utilization, unified-7d-utilization, and related fields from ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/last-headers.json (populated after each Claude API call). On cold start, run a minimal probe to populate the file.
acceptance_criteria: _check_headers reads ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/last-headers.json
Parses anthropic-ratelimit-unified-5h-utilization, unified-7d-utilization, unified-status, unified-*-reset, is_using_overage
Computes billing_mode from header presence (subscription vs overage vs api)
On cold start (file missing), runs `claude -p ok --max-turns 1 --model haiku` once and re-reads
auto mode prefers headers over oauth and cli
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase7.sh: _check_headers with valid last-headers.json returns utilization correctly
bin/test-phase7.sh: _check_headers with missing file triggers cold-start probe
bin/test-phase7.sh: billing_mode=subscription when unified-* headers present and is_using_overage=false
bin/test-phase7.sh: billing_mode=overage when is_using_overage=true
bin/test-phase7.sh: billing_mode=api when no unified-* headers but ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is set

title: Fix or remove broken OAuth fallback in pipeline-quota-check
description: Line 35 uses `jq -r '.access_token // empty'` but the macOS Keychain credential format nests under `.claudeAiOauth.accessToken` (see reference lib/usage.sh:54). _check_oauth always silently fails and falls through to the CLI probe. Either fix the JSON path OR remove OAuth fallback entirely in favor of header-based detection.
acceptance_criteria: Either: _check_oauth correctly parses .claudeAiOauth.accessToken from Keychain
Or: _check_oauth is removed entirely and auto mode uses headers-only
Decision documented inline with a comment explaining why
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase7.sh: OAuth fallback (if kept) extracts token from correct JSON path
bin/test-phase7.sh: auto mode prefers headers, then falls back appropriately

title: Remove quota-burning CLI probe fallback
description: _check_cli at pipeline-quota-check:120-154 runs `claude -p ok --max-turns 1 --model haiku` to detect rate limiting. This burns a turn from the user's quota on every check — anti-pattern. Remove this path; if header detection fails, return an explicit error rather than probing.
acceptance_criteria: _check_cli method is removed or only runs once for cold-start population (then never again)
auto mode does not fall back to CLI probe after header detection fails
Failure returns an explicit error with instructions, not silent safe defaults
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase7.sh: auto mode does not invoke claude CLI when headers file exists
bin/test-phase7.sh: explicit error returned when both headers and oauth fail

title: Add reconcile_staging_with_develop to pipeline-branch staging-init
description: The old pipeline's repository.sh reconciles staging with develop at every run start. pipeline-branch staging-init only pulls staging without syncing develop's changes. Add an explicit reconcile step: fetch develop, ff-merge staging onto develop (if ancestor), fallback to merge with conflict handling.
acceptance_criteria: pipeline-branch staging-init (after pulling staging) fast-forwards staging onto develop if develop exists
Falls back to non-ff merge if fast-forward is not possible
Aborts cleanly on merge conflicts (not leaving staging in dirty state)
Returns diagnostic JSON about reconciliation result
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase4.sh: staging-init when develop is ancestor of staging — no-op
bin/test-phase4.sh: staging-init when develop is ahead — fast-forwards staging
bin/test-phase4.sh: staging-init when staging has commits not in develop — merges
bin/test-phase4.sh: staging-init when merge would conflict — aborts without leaving dirty state

title: Fix spec-generator worktree handoff
description: spec-generator has isolation: worktree, meaning its spec.md + tasks.json outputs are written to an ephemeral worktree. The orchestrator then calls pipeline-validate-tasks on a path that doesn't exist in its view. Choose one: (a) drop worktree isolation from spec-generator and have it write to the project dir directly, or (b) add an explicit pipeline-spec-publish script that cherry-picks or copies the spec out of the worktree.
acceptance_criteria: Spec files written by spec-generator are reachable by the orchestrator
The handoff mechanism is deterministic (either scripted or enforced by a non-worktree isolation)
The chosen approach is documented in agents/spec-generator.md and agents/pipeline-orchestrator.md with a clear spec_dir path contract
pipeline-validate-tasks never fails with 'tasks.json not found' when the spec-generator exited successfully
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase8.sh: orchestrator.md references the spec handoff mechanism explicitly
bin/test-phase8.sh: spec-generator.md documents output path contract
bin/test-integration.sh (new): end-to-end spec-generator → orchestrator flow finds tasks.json at expected location

title: Implement commit_spec_to_staging equivalent
description: After spec generation and before task execution, the orchestrator must commit the spec directory to the staging branch so that task-executors (branching from staging) can see the spec. The old pipeline had commit_spec_to_staging() in repository.sh. Port the equivalent as a bin script invoked by the orchestrator.
acceptance_criteria: pipeline-branch commit-spec <spec-dir> commits the spec directory to staging with message 'chore: add spec directory <path>'
Idempotent: skipped if no uncommitted changes in spec dir
Orchestrator calls this after pipeline-validate-tasks succeeds and before the execution loop starts
orchestrator.md documents this step
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase4.sh: commit-spec commits untracked spec files to staging
bin/test-phase4.sh: commit-spec is idempotent (no-op if nothing to commit)
bin/test-phase4.sh: commit-spec leaves staging branch checked out

title: Pass spec path explicitly through orchestrator to task-executor
description: Currently the orchestrator has no deterministic record of where the spec dir lives. Store it in state.json under .spec.path (field already exists in pipeline-init output) and include it in every task-executor prompt via pipeline-build-prompt.
acceptance_criteria: Orchestrator writes spec.path to state.json after spec-generation phase
pipeline-build-prompt reads spec.path from state if not passed as argument
Task-executor prompt includes the absolute spec path
Resume path: orchestrator reads .spec.path from state instead of rediscovering
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase3.sh: pipeline-build-prompt reads .spec.path from state when --spec-path not given
bin/test-phase3.sh: prompt output contains absolute spec path
bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-state write .spec.path persists correctly

title: Port full settings.autonomous.json from old pipeline
description: Decision 14 in 05-decisions.md claims the plugin ships the autonomous settings template 'as an exact copy'. The current templates/settings.autonomous.json is a radically reduced subset with no deny-list, 1 hook, no MCP permissions, no quality gate enforcement. Port the full file from ~/Projects/dark-factory/templates/settings.autonomous.json.
acceptance_criteria: templates/settings.autonomous.json includes all hooks from the old pipeline: .claude access block, branch protection via git branch --show-current, protected-files (.env, secrets, migrations), SQL safety, dangerous patterns, pre-commit-check, pre-push-check
Post-tool hooks: prettier auto-format, related test runner, audit log
Stop hook: full vitest suite
Full deny-list from the old file
DARK_FACTORY_AUTONOMOUS_MODE=1 env var remains
Paths that reference user hooks (~/.claude/hooks/*) are preserved as-is since the plugin inherits the user environment
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase9.sh: template has PreToolUse deny hooks for .env, migrations, secrets
bin/test-phase9.sh: template has Stop hook with vitest runner
bin/test-phase9.sh: template has PostToolUse prettier hook
bin/test-phase9.sh: template has deny-list with rm -rf, git push --force, etc.
bin/test-phase9.sh: jq -e . validates the template

title: Update run.md materialization for new hook set
description: commands/run.md currently materializes a single PreToolUse hook path. With the full template from task_04_01, materialization must update every plugin-owned hook path (branch-protection, etc.) AND leave user-owned hook paths (~/.claude/hooks/*) alone.
acceptance_criteria: The materialization jq expression correctly rewrites only plugin-relative hook paths
User-owned ~/.claude/hooks/* paths are untouched
Materialized settings file validates as JSON and loads correctly via --settings flag
The rewrite is idempotent
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase9.sh: materialization rewrites plugin-relative hook paths to absolute
bin/test-phase9.sh: materialization preserves ~/.claude/hooks/* paths verbatim
bin/test-phase9.sh: materialized file is valid JSON

title: Verify test-phase9 covers new template structure
description: test-phase9.sh was built against the stripped-down template. After porting the full file, every assertion in test-phase9 needs to be re-verified and new assertions added for the expanded hook set.
acceptance_criteria: All existing test-phase9 assertions still pass
New assertions cover each hook in the ported template
Running bin/test-phase9.sh exits 0
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase9.sh: all pre-existing tests still pass
bin/test-phase9.sh: new assertions for each ported hook

title: Anchor grep in pipeline-branch staging-init
description: pipeline-branch:15 uses `git ls-remote --heads origin staging | grep -q staging`. grep matches any line containing 'staging' including refs/heads/staging-v2. Use `grep -qE 'refs/heads/staging$'`.
acceptance_criteria: Grep is anchored to exact ref path
Branches named staging-v2, pre-staging, etc. do not cause false matches
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase4.sh: staging-init does not false-match on refs/heads/staging-v2
bin/test-phase4.sh: staging-init correctly detects existing staging branch
bin/test-phase4.sh: staging-init creates staging when only staging-v2 exists

title: Handle existing branch in pipeline-branch create
description: pipeline-branch:45 runs `git checkout -b "$name" "$base" &>/dev/null`. If the branch exists (common on resume), git fails, error is suppressed, JSON claims action='created'. Check for existence first and either check out + rebase (resume path) or fail loudly.
acceptance_criteria: If branch exists locally: check it out and rebase onto base; set action='resumed' in output
If branch exists remotely only: fetch + track + rebase; action='resumed'
If branch does not exist: create from base; action='created'
Rebase failure sets action='conflict' and returns non-zero exit code
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase4.sh: create against existing branch returns action=resumed
bin/test-phase4.sh: create against non-existing branch returns action=created
bin/test-phase4.sh: create with rebase conflict returns action=conflict with exit 1
bin/test-phase4.sh: error output is no longer suppressed

title: Implement multi-commit rebase loop in pipeline-wait-pr
description: pipeline-wait-pr:129 runs `git rebase --continue` once. For a PR with multiple commits each with their own conflicts, only the first gets resolved. The old pipeline's orchestrator.sh had a loop up to 30 rounds. Port that semantics.
acceptance_criteria: The rebase loop runs up to 30 rounds, auto-resolving each conflict
If a round surfaces an unsafe file, the loop aborts and exits 4
If rebase --continue produces an empty commit, it's skipped via --skip
The loop terminates when rebase is clean
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase4.sh: multi-commit rebase with sequential conflicts resolves all rounds
bin/test-phase4.sh: rebase aborts cleanly on first unsafe conflict
bin/test-phase4.sh: empty commit after resolution is skipped via --skip
bin/test-phase4.sh: loop terminates at 30 rounds with error

title: Implement package.json 3-way merge in pipeline-wait-pr rebase
description: Line 42 comment in bin/pipeline-wait-pr promises 'package.json → attempt 3-way merge' but the implementation doesn't handle it. Port the old pipeline's logic: extract :1/:2/:3 stages, jq --indent 4 normalize, git merge-file -p, write result, git add.
acceptance_criteria: package.json conflicts during rebase are resolved via 3-way merge using jq-normalized stages
If the 3-way merge has semantic conflicts, rebase aborts cleanly
Merge result is committed via git add
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase4.sh: package.json formatting-only conflict auto-resolves
bin/test-phase4.sh: package.json semantic conflict aborts rebase
bin/test-phase4.sh: package.json with both dep additions on both sides merges correctly

title: Handle mergeable=UNKNOWN in pipeline-wait-pr
description: pipeline-wait-pr:170 checks mergeable == CONFLICTING but GitHub also returns UNKNOWN while computing. The script proceeds to CI checks in that case without re-polling mergeability, causing unnecessary work and log spam.
acceptance_criteria: mergeable=UNKNOWN causes the poll loop to sleep and retry mergeability fetch
Max retry count before treating UNKNOWN as CONFLICTING, to avoid infinite loop
Log messages distinguish UNKNOWN (waiting) from CONFLICTING (taking action)
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase4.sh: UNKNOWN mergeable causes retry without rebase attempt
bin/test-phase4.sh: UNKNOWN after max retries is treated as conflict

title: Check PR merge state before deleting branches
description: pipeline-cleanup --delete-branches:65 deletes branch when task_status == 'done'. 'done' means review approved, not PR merged. If the user is on humanReviewLevel >= 1 (PR approval), the PR is still open when the task is done. Deleting the branch breaks the PR.
acceptance_criteria: --delete-branches calls `gh pr view <pr_number> --json state,mergedAt` for each task
Only branches belonging to PRs in state=MERGED are deleted
Open PRs are skipped with warning (listed in output warnings)
Closed unmerged PRs are skipped
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase5.sh: --delete-branches skips branch with open PR
bin/test-phase5.sh: --delete-branches deletes branch with merged PR
bin/test-phase5.sh: --delete-branches skips branch with closed unmerged PR
bin/test-phase5.sh: output includes warnings for skipped branches

title: pipeline-state resume-point follows execution_order
description: pipeline-state resume-point uses `.tasks | to_entries[] | first` which depends on jq object iteration order (non-deterministic in some jq versions). Resume should iterate execution_order from state.json and return the first entry whose task is not done/failed.
acceptance_criteria: resume-point reads .execution_order from state
Iterates in array order, returns first task_id whose status is not done or failed
Falls back to old behavior with warning if execution_order is missing (pre-seeding state)
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase1.sh: resume-point returns first incomplete task by execution_order
bin/test-phase1.sh: resume-point respects parallel_group ordering (returns earlier group first)
bin/test-phase1.sh: resume-point skips done and failed tasks
bin/test-phase1.sh: resume-point returns empty with exit 1 when all tasks done
bin/test-phase1.sh: resume-point works without execution_order (legacy)

title: Expand stop-gate.sh to handle all task statuses
description: hooks/stop-gate.sh:45-61 only handles executing|reviewing|done|failed. Missing: pending, interrupted, needs_human_review, ci_fixing. Uncovered statuses fall through to 'all_done=false' and are miscategorized. In particular, needs_human_review tasks get marked as interrupted.
acceptance_criteria: All status values defined in pipeline-state are handled explicitly
needs_human_review tasks do not transition to interrupted
ci_fixing tasks are marked as interrupted on session end (they need re-start)
Pending tasks leave run in 'running' state (not final) — orchestrator will resume
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase1.sh: stop-gate with needs_human_review task keeps status=needs_human_review
bin/test-phase1.sh: stop-gate with ci_fixing task sets status=interrupted
bin/test-phase1.sh: stop-gate with all pending tasks leaves run status=interrupted
bin/test-phase1.sh: stop-gate with mixed done + pending marks run partial

title: subagent-stop-gate iterates all executing tasks
description: hooks/subagent-stop-gate.sh:52-54 uses `first // empty` to check only one executing task. With parallel task-executors, other executing tasks are silently skipped. Iterate all executing tasks.
acceptance_criteria: All executing tasks are checked, not just the first
Warnings are emitted per-task (not aggregated)
git log checks run against each task's worktree path (from state), not orchestrator cwd
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase1.sh: subagent-stop-gate iterates 3 parallel executing tasks
bin/test-phase1.sh: subagent-stop-gate emits warning per task with missing commits
bin/test-phase1.sh: subagent-stop-gate reads worktree path from state

title: pipeline-init ownership check for current symlink
description: pipeline-init:99 runs `rm -f "$current_link"` unconditionally. If another run is active, this silently breaks it. Before removing, readlink the target and check whether the linked run is in a non-terminal state; if so, refuse to overwrite (use --force to override).
acceptance_criteria: pipeline-init refuses to overwrite 'current' symlink pointing to a run with status=running
Returns exit 1 with clear error mentioning the active run
--force flag allows override
If target is a terminated run (completed/failed/partial/interrupted), safe to replace without --force
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-init fails when current points to running run
bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-init --force replaces running current
bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-init replaces current pointing to completed run
bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-init replaces dangling symlink

title: Make pipeline-lock recover atomic
description: bin/pipeline-lock:109-110 does `rm -f LOCK_FILE; printf > LOCK_FILE`. Between rm and printf, another process can acquire the lock. Use mv-based atomic replacement instead.
acceptance_criteria: Lock recovery is atomic: write new lock to tmp file, then mv -f
No window where the lock file is absent
Concurrent recovery attempts: exactly one succeeds
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase1.sh: lock recover is atomic under rapid concurrent calls
bin/test-phase1.sh: lock recover from dead PID produces valid new lock

title: pipeline-circuit-breaker deducts pause time from runtime
description: bin/pipeline-circuit-breaker:37 computes runtime from wall clock (now - started_at). Rate-limit waits count toward the 360-minute runtime limit. The old pipeline tracked pause time separately and deducted it. Add a .circuit_breaker.pause_minutes field to state, incremented when model-router returns action=wait, and subtract it from runtime.
acceptance_criteria: state.json includes .circuit_breaker.pause_minutes (default 0)
Orchestrator increments this via pipeline-state when a wait occurs
pipeline-circuit-breaker computes runtime = (now - started_at) - pause_minutes*60
A paused run does not trip the circuit breaker due to wall-clock time alone
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase1.sh: circuit-breaker with pause_minutes=180 allows run to continue past 360min wall clock
bin/test-phase1.sh: circuit-breaker trips at 360min effective runtime
bin/test-phase1.sh: pipeline-state write .circuit_breaker.pause_minutes increments correctly

title: Fix pipeline-build-prompt prior-work detection
description: bin/pipeline-build-prompt:93-105 runs `git log staging..HEAD` in orchestrator cwd, which is the project dir, not the task worktree. The prior-work detection is effectively broken. Accept a --worktree-path argument and cd into it (or --git-dir) before running git log.
acceptance_criteria: --worktree-path flag allows the orchestrator to specify the task's worktree
git log runs against that path via `git -C <path>` or `--git-dir`
Empty worktree path skips prior-work section (no-op)
Documented in orchestrator.md execution sequence
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase3.sh: build-prompt with --worktree-path detects commits in that worktree
bin/test-phase3.sh: build-prompt without --worktree-path skips prior-work section
bin/test-phase3.sh: prior-work section includes commit messages from correct worktree

title: Anchor VERDICT regex in pipeline-parse-review
description: pipeline-parse-review:38 uses `grep 'VERDICT:' | grep -oE 'APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|NEEDS_DISCUSSION'`. A reviewer quoting code that mentions these tokens corrupts their own verdict. Anchor to line-starting `**VERDICT:**` (the exact format specified by the review-protocol skill).
acceptance_criteria: VERDICT extraction anchors to `^\*\*VERDICT:\*\*`
A reviewer quoting APPROVE in a code block does not corrupt the verdict
The skill's documented output format is the only recognized format
Parse failure is loud (exit 1, clear error)
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase6.sh: verdict extraction ignores APPROVE mentioned inside code fence
bin/test-phase6.sh: verdict extraction ignores REQUEST_CHANGES inside a bullet
bin/test-phase6.sh: verdict correctly extracted from **VERDICT:** REQUEST_CHANGES line
bin/test-phase6.sh: missing VERDICT exits 1 (no silent default)

title: Add deterministic quality gate runner
description: Quality gate layers 1 and 2 (02-quality-and-config.md) rely entirely on user hooks which the plugin no longer ships by default. Add a pipeline-quality-gate script that runs lint/typecheck/test via the detected package manager. The orchestrator invokes it after task-executor returns, before the adversarial review round.
acceptance_criteria: New bin/pipeline-quality-gate script runs <pkg-manager> lint, typecheck, test
Emits JSON output: {passed: bool, lint: {...}, typecheck: {...}, test: {...}}
Exits 0 on pass, 1 on any failure
Package manager is detected via detect_pkg_manager (pipeline-lib.sh)
Detects missing scripts gracefully (e.g., no typecheck script — skip with warning, not fail)
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase6.sh: pipeline-quality-gate passes when lint/typecheck/test all pass
bin/test-phase6.sh: pipeline-quality-gate fails when lint fails
bin/test-phase6.sh: pipeline-quality-gate fails when test fails
bin/test-phase6.sh: pipeline-quality-gate skips gracefully when typecheck script missing
bin/test-phase6.sh: detects pnpm/npm/yarn/bun correctly

title: Update orchestrator.md to call quality gate runner explicitly
description: Add a step to the execution sequence in agents/pipeline-orchestrator.md: after format+lint auto-fix, call pipeline-quality-gate. On failure, set TASK_FAILURE_TYPE=quality_gate and retry the task-executor with the gate output.
acceptance_criteria: Step 11 in execution sequence calls pipeline-quality-gate
Quality gate failure triggers task retry with quality_gate failure type
Documented in orchestrator.md
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase8.sh: orchestrator.md references pipeline-quality-gate

title: Fix configure.md jq setpath for nested keys
description: commands/configure.md:97-99 uses `jq --arg k "<key>" '.[$k] = $v'`. This treats $k as a literal object key, not a nested path, so setting 'circuitBreaker.maxTasks' creates a top-level `"circuitBreaker.maxTasks"` key with a dot in its name. Rewrite to split the key into a path array and use `setpath($path; $v)`.
acceptance_criteria: Setting circuitBreaker.maxTasks creates nested path, not flat key
Example jq invocation uses `setpath(($key | split(".")); $v)` or equivalent
Documentation shows the correct pattern
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase9.sh: configure.md example writes to nested path
bin/test-phase9.sh (if practical to exercise): running the jq one-liner updates a nested key

title: Fix configure.md string vs argjson handling
description: commands/configure.md:98 uses `--argjson v <value>` uniformly. String values (ollamaUrl, model) fail because they're not valid JSON. Distinguish: numeric/boolean → --argjson; string → --arg. Document the distinction.
acceptance_criteria: Documentation and example use --arg for strings, --argjson for numbers/booleans
Writing localLlm.ollamaUrl works correctly
Writing review.routineRounds as a number works correctly
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase9.sh: configure.md example distinguishes string vs numeric arg types

title: Align config key names with PRD or update PRD
description: The PRD uses 'quality.holdoutPercent' but plugin.json uses 'holdout.percent'. PRD uses 'quality.mutationScoreTarget' but plugin.json uses 'mutationTesting.scoreThreshold'. Pick one naming and fix the other. Coordinate with task_14_02 (PRD honesty updates).
acceptance_criteria: plugin.json, PRD, scripts, configure.md, and agent references all use the same keys
grep -r 'holdoutPercent\|holdout.percent' shows consistent usage
Migration note added if existing users need to rename keys
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase9.sh: plugin.json keys match PRD documentation

title: Add flock to run-tracker.sh sequence counter
description: hooks/run-tracker.sh:39 computes seq_num via `wc -l < audit.jsonl` then increments. Two concurrent PostToolUse invocations compute the same seq_num and append duplicates. Use flock (2) on the audit file around the read-count-append section.
acceptance_criteria: Sequence increment is serialized via flock
Concurrent invocations produce strictly monotonic sequence numbers
Handles case where flock is not available (Linux vs macOS — use a portable alternative if needed)
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase1.sh: 50 concurrent run-tracker invocations produce 50 unique sequence numbers
bin/test-phase1.sh: sequence numbers are strictly monotonic after concurrent run

title: Implement real hash chain OR remove tamper-evident claim
description: hooks/run-tracker.sh:30 hashes the current entry's params, not a chain. 02-quality-and-config.md:629-634 claims tamper-evidence via hash chain. Either: (a) implement the chain (read last line's hash, compute SHA256(prev_hash + this_entry), store), or (b) remove the claim from documentation.
acceptance_criteria: Either: audit.jsonl entries include prev_hash, and modifying any historical entry breaks the chain downstream
Or: 02-quality-and-config.md does not claim tamper-evidence, and the PRD notes hash chain is a P2 future enhancement
Decision documented
tests_to_write: bin/test-phase1.sh: each audit entry's hash depends on the previous entry (if chain implemented)
bin/test-phase1.sh: modifying a historical entry breaks downstream hash verification (if chain implemented)

title: Add current-branch awareness to branch-protection.sh
description: hooks/branch-protection.sh only pattern-matches command text. `git reset --hard HEAD~1` run from main is not blocked because the command doesn't literally contain 'main'. Port the old pipeline's behavior: inspect the current branch via `git -C <cwd> branch --show-current` and block Write/Edit/destructive git ops targeting the branch when it's in PROTECTED_BRANCHES.
acceptance_criteria: When current branch is main/master/develop, git reset --hard is blocked regardless of command text
When current branch is staging or feat/*, git reset --hard is allowed
git rebase on protected branches is blocked
Output 'decision: block' JSON matches hook contract

title: Robust gh API owner/repo resolution in pipeline-gh-comment
description: bin/pipeline-gh-comment:161 uses `gh api "repos/{owner}/{repo}/..."` which relies on gh's cwd-based autodetection. Orchestrator may call this from a worktree or outside a git repo. Resolve the repo explicitly via `gh repo view --json nameWithOwner` at script start and use the concrete path.

gh api uses the concrete path, not the {owner}/{repo} placeholder
Script fails loudly if repo resolution fails

title: Add fsync to pipeline-lib.sh atomic_write
description: bin/pipeline-lib.sh atomic_write does `printf > tmp; mv -f tmp target`. The write may not be fsync'd before the rename. On crash, target could be a dangling inode or empty file. Add `sync` or use `install` which fsyncs.
acceptance_criteria: atomic_write ensures content is durable before rename
Acceptable: sync after printf, or use install -m 644
